sanskrit:
चिन्तामपरिमेयां च प्रलयान्तामुपाश्रिताः।
कामोपभोगपरमा एतावदिति निश्चिताः॥
transliteration:
cintām aparimeyāṃ ca pralayāntām upāśritāḥ |
kāmopabhoga-paramā etāvad iti niścitāḥ ||
literal_meaning: Having taken shelter of anxiety without measure up to the point of death, convinced that sense-enjoyment is the highest goal, they decide 'this alone is it.'
contextual_meaning: A mind trapped in compulsive planning for endless gratification, mistaking sensory satiation for life’s purpose, remains anxious until death.
psychological_state: Compulsive consumerism driven by death-denial and hedonic treadmill.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling online shops at 2 a.m. with maxed-out credit cards, chasing the next purchase to quiet an inner hum of dread.
anchor_text: Anxiety ends when we stop chasing pleasures and start living with purpose.
